title: Matrices : DPP 01
subject: Mathematics
questions: A matrix having equal number of rows and columns is called:
The transpose of matrix A is denoted by:
If A = [aᵢⱼ] is a square matrix where aᵢⱼ = aⱼᵢ for all i, j, then A is:
Order of matrix AB where A is 2×3 and B is 3×4:
If A is a matrix of order m×n, then order of Aᵀ is:
A matrix in which all non-diagonal elements are zero is called:
If A² = A, then matrix A is called:
The additive inverse of matrix A is:
For a null matrix O, A + O equals:
A square matrix A is invertible if and only if:
explanations: A square matrix has equal number of rows and columns (m = n).
Transpose is denoted by both Aᵀ and A', so both A and B are correct.
When aᵢⱼ = aⱼᵢ for all i,j, matrix is symmetric.
A(2×3) × B(3×4) = AB(2×4). Inner dimensions must match.
Transpose swaps rows and columns: m×n becomes n×m.
A diagonal matrix has zeros everywhere except the main diagonal.
If A² = A, matrix is called idempotent.
Additive inverse of A is -A, since A + (-A) = O.
A + O = A for any matrix A (O is additive identity).
A square matrix is invertible (non-singular) if and only if |A| ≠ 0.
